title: Te Aromatawai Taiao o Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku / The Mangakōtukutuku Stream Environmental Assessment
subject: Mathematics
culturalContext: Students will investigate the health of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, a significant waterway flowing through Ngāti Kahungunu rohe. This stream holds cultural importance as a traditional food gathering site and is mentioned in local pūrākau (stories) about the taniwha Hinekorako. The assessment will connect mathematical analysis with the Māori concept of kaitiakitanga (guardianship) and the college's role as environmental stewards.
learningObjectives: Calculate and interpret water quality indices using statistical measures (mean, median, mode) from stream testing data
Create and analyze graphical representations (scatter plots, line graphs) of environmental data to identify trends and correlations
Apply geometric principles to calculate stream flow rates and catchment areas using cross-sectional measurements
Develop mathematical models to predict environmental impacts and propose data-informed conservation strategies

activities.title: Whakawhanaungatanga ki Te Awa (Connecting with the Stream)
activities.description: Students visit Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku with local kaumātua who share pūrākau about the stream's significance. Students conduct initial observations and learn proper protocols for engaging with the waterway as kaitiaki.

activities.title: Tātari Raraunga Wai (Water Data Analysis)
activities.description: Using water testing kits, students collect pH, turbidity, nitrate, and temperature data at multiple points along the stream. They organize this data into spreadsheets and calculate central tendency measures to establish baseline water quality indices.

activities.title: Hoahoa Awe (Graphical Representation)
activities.description: Students create scatter plots comparing water quality parameters, line graphs showing changes over time, and bar graphs comparing different sampling locations. They analyze these visualizations to identify correlations and concerning trends.

activities.title: Tātari Pūtaiao Pāngarau (Mathematical Scientific Analysis)
activities.description: Students apply geometric formulas to calculate stream cross-sectional area and flow rate. They use algebraic equations to model potential pollution scenarios and their mathematical impact on water quality parameters.

activities.title: Whakatau Kaitiaki (Guardian Decision-Making)
activities.description: Working in groups, students develop mathematical proposals for stream restoration, calculating required resources, cost projections, and predicted environmental outcomes using their collected data and models.

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu kaumātua and environmental knowledge holders
Water testing kits and flow measurement equipment from NIWA
Te Awa Tupua (Whanganui River Claims Settlement) Act 2017 as legislative context
Mangakōtukutuku Stream historical water quality data from Hawke's Bay Regional Council

tasks: Complete water quality data analysis workbook with statistical calculations
Create a comprehensive set of graphs visualizing stream health indicators
Develop a mathematical model predicting the impact of a proposed conservation intervention
Present findings to a panel including school leadership and local iwi representatives

nzcAlignment: Mathematics and Statistics Level 5: Statistical investigation, Statistical literacy, Equations and expressions, Geometric representations
Science Level 5: Planet Earth and Beyond - investigating environmental issues
Social Sciences Level 5: Understanding how communities make decisions and participate in action
Key Competencies: Thinking, Using language, symbols, and texts, Participating and contributing
